title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Blocked Nose
intro: Herbal teas for Blocked nose are a natural remedy used to alleviate nasal congestion and sinus pressure. They work by loosening mucus, reducing inflammation, and promoting drainage, providing relief from the discomfort associated with a blocked nose. Some effective herbal teas for blocked nose include eucalyptus, which contains decongestant properties that help clear airways; peppermint, which helps to thin mucus and promote drainage; ginger, which reduces inflammation and eases congestion; and thyme, which has antimicrobial properties that combat sinus infections. Additionally, other herbal teas like elderflower, licorice root, and rosehip tea can also be used to treat blocked nose due to their decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat blocked nose and respiratory issues due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus, including eucalyptol, cineole, and flavonoids, help to break down mucus and reduce congestion, thereby relieving nasal blockage and sinus pressure. By acting as a natural expectorant and mucolytic agent, Eucalyptus globulus teas facilitate the removal of excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and promoting overall respiratory health. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas for blocked nose include rapid relief from congestion, reduced sinus pressure, and improved respiratory function, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Boil the water in a pot.
Add the dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink the tea while it's still warm to help relieve a blocked nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Turkish archives of otorhinolaryptology", Eucalyptus globulus teas, part of the herbal preparation Sinulan forte, were found to be effective in treating nasal congestion associated with blocked nose, with no adverse effects reported.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate the symptoms of a blocked nose, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and an increased heart rate in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to follow the recommended dosage and steeping time, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and monitor your body's response, especially if you have pre-existing conditions such as high blood pressure or sensitive stomach.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Use 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus tea leaves per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to release active ingredients.
Inhale the vapor for 5-10 minutes after drinking the tea.
Repeat daily for up to 7 days for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat the blocked nose ailment, also known as nasal congestion, due to their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, play a crucial role in reducing inflammation and promoting the production of mucus, which helps to expel excess mucus and alleviate nasal congestion. Overall, Echinacea purpurea teas offer several benefits in treating blocked nose, including relief from congestion, reduced inflammation, and improved respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Echinacea flowers in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Echinacea flowers.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve a blocked nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Journal of family practice", Echinacea purpurea teas may be beneficial for the early treatment of acute upper respiratory infections, which may include symptoms such as a blocked nose, as evidenced by the generally positive results from eight out of nine treatment trials.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can cause possible side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions, particularly when consumed in large quantities or by individuals with pre-existing health conditions. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to use Echinacea purpurea teas in moderation, avoid mixing them with other medications or herbal remedies, and monitor your body's response closely, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Increase dosage to 2 tablespoons if symptoms persist after 3 days.
Use Echinacea purpurea tea for 5-7 days to see noticeable improvements.
Combine with other remedies like steam inhalation for better results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, commonly known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat blocked nose ailments due to their decongestant properties. The active constituents responsible for this effect include gingerols and shogaols, which possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion and promote the expulsion of mucus. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from blocked nose. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat blocked nose include its ability to provide rapid relief, ease congestion, and promote respiratory health without any significant side effects.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger, and a tea strainer.
Peel and grate 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add grated ginger to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while it's warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for blocked nose may offer relief due to its potential efflux pump inhibitory activity, as lariciresinol from Z. officinale has been identified as a potential inhibitor that could lead to effective killing of drug-resistant bacteria, potentially including those responsible for respiratory disorders.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat a blocked nose ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and heartburn in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or on an empty stomach. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and eat a meal or snack before drinking it to minimize the risk of stomach discomfort.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea, 2-3 times a day.
Start with small dose of 1/2 teaspoon of ginger powder per cup.
Gradually increase dose to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed.
Continue using tea for 3-5 days to clear nasal congestion.
Monitor and adjust frequency of usage based on relief and comfort.

The benefits of Rosmarinus officinalis teas in treating a blocked nose include rapid relief from congestion, improved respiratory function, and a soothing effect on the nasal passages.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective remedy for treating a blocked nose, but it may also cause some side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of volatile oils. Repeat drinking 1 cup of warm tea 3 times a day.

Drink tea for 3-5 days to help relieve blocked nose.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the blocked nose ailment, also known as rhinitis, due to their decongestant and expectorant properties. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat a blocked nose include relief from nasal congestion, reduced inflammation, and improved respiratory function, making it a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.

Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in a cup.

Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Salvia officinalis per cup of boiling water
Take the tea 1 hour before meals for better absorption
Continue for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvements

Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or a strainer to remove any remaining Piper nigrum particles.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemon grass tea, have been traditionally used to treat a blocked nose due to their decongestant and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents, including citral and geraniol, help to relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages. This herbal preparation works by increasing blood flow and reducing the thickness of mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving sinus pressure. Regular consumption of Cymbopogon citratus teas has been shown to provide quick relief from blocked nose symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the water.
Drink the tea hot or warm, 2-3 times a day to help relieve a blocked nose.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate the symptoms of a blocked nose, a common issue associated with respiratory congestion and sinus pressure. The herbal preparation contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and promote sinus drainage. By increasing blood flow to the nasal passages and mucous membranes, Ginkgo biloba teas can help to thin mucus and improve respiratory function, making it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat a blocked nose include improved respiratory health, reduced sinus pressure, and a general sense of well-being.

Add the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate a blocked nose.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil infusions, have been traditionally used to treat blocked noses due to their decongestant and expectorant properties. These properties help to relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages. The bioactive constituents of Melaleuca alternifolia, including eucalyptol, cineole, and terpinen-4-ol, work together to provide these benefits and help to treat blocked noses by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel. Regular consumption of Melaleuca alternifolia teas may provide relief from nasal congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, for a sweeter taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to help relieve a blocked nose.
Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours and reheat as needed.
